title: Internal DNS not working from Podman Machine on Windows (WSL2)
body:
### Issue Description

I'm using Podman on Windows via Podman Desktop and experience issues with the Fedora machine installed on WSL2. I have no VPN installed.

The machine gets the correct DNS configuration from WSL2. The `/etc/resolv.conf` file looks correct and it matches what is used on my other VMs that all work correctly (including a Fedora machine installed independently from Podman). 

I can reach services from the public Internet, but I can't seem to resolve names from the internal corporate DNS. Tools in the Podman machine that fail in resolving names include `curl`, `ping`, `wget `and `podman`. Strangely, it works correctly when using the `host` tool (installed from `bind-utils`).

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Install a Podman machine on WSL2 using Podman version 4.6.0 (it doesn't matter if rootless or not)
2. Run `podman machine ssh`
3. Run `curl google.com`. It works.
4. Run `curl myservice.corporate.local`. It doesn't work.

### Describe the results you received

**From inside the Podman machine (`podman machine ssh`).**

`podman` doesn't work

```
$ podman pull myservice.corporate.local/my-image:4.2.0
Trying to pull myservice.corporate.local/my-image:4.2.0...
Error: initializing source docker://myservice.corporate.local/my-image:4.2.0: pinging container registry myservice.corporate.local: Get "https://myservice.corporate.local/v2/": dial tcp: lookup myservice.corporate.local: Temporary failure in name resolution
```

`curl` doesn't work

```
$ curl myservice.corporate.local
curl: (6) Could not resolve host: myservice.corporate.local
```

`ping` doesn't work

```
$ ping myservice.corporate.local
ping: myservice.corporate.local: Temporary failure in name resolution
```

`host` **works*!*

```
$ host myservice.corporate.local
myservice.corporate.local is an alias for vmz-blah01.corporate.local.
vmz-blah01.corporate.local has address 172.18.16.12
```

**From the Windows host**

`wsl` **works*!*

```
$ wsl -d podman-machine-default curl myservice.corporate.local
200 OK
```

### Describe the results you expected

I expect to be run all the commands in the previous section successfully.

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

No VPN installed.
I'm using Podman 4.6.0 with Podman Desktop 1.2.1.

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body: It seems to be a duplicate of https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/18911, where a temporary workaround as been described. See: https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/18911#issuecomment-1659269933
